F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: After a long time without visiting, the protagonist visits the person’s home to reconnect.

GPT-5.2.name: Mary Atong
GPT-5.2.story:
Mary realized she had not been to her former neighbor’s home in Juba for many months. One morning she bought a small sack of sugar and a bundle of tea from the market and walked toward the old block where the neighbor lived.

At the gate she called softly, “Mother Nyandeng, it is Mary.” A woman came out from the doorway and looked at her.

“Ah, Mary,” the woman said.

Mary stepped forward and offered her hand. “I have been far,” she said. “I came to see you.”

They sat on chairs under the shade cloth. Mary asked, “How is your health? How are the children?” The woman answered and called to a younger boy inside, “Bring chairs.”

Mary listened and nodded, then asked about people she used to see on the lane. She spoke about her work and where she had been staying.

After a while the neighbor stood and went inside, returning with water in a jug. Mary accepted the cup and drank.

Before leaving, Mary stood, adjusted her scarf, and said, “I will pass again next week.” She shook hands again at the gate, and the woman held Mary’s hand for a moment before letting go as Mary stepped onto the road.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they cannot visit yet, the protagonist sends a clear message explaining the delay and sets a date to visit.

GPT-5.2.name: Aluel Nyandeng
GPT-5.2.story:
Aluel saw the missed calls from her older cousin in Juba and checked the calendar on her phone. She was still in Wau and could not travel that week. She opened a message and typed slowly so it was clear.

“Brother, I know it has been a long time,” she wrote. “I did not come because I am still here with my sister and the travel is not possible for me now.”

A few minutes later, she added another line: “I will return to Juba on the 18th. I will visit you on Saturday the 20th in the afternoon, after 3.” She pressed send.

Her cousin replied, “Okay, we will be at home.”

Aluel sent back, “Thank you. I will call you when I arrive.”

On the 18th, after the bus reached Juba, Aluel called him from outside the station. “I arrived,” she said. “I will not come today; I will come Saturday as I wrote.”

On Saturday the 20th, she bought a small packet of tea and walked to his compound. At the gate she called out his name and waited until he came toward her before she entered.

When he opened the gate, she said, “I came as we planned,” and stepped inside while he led her toward the chairs.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist makes the reconnecting visit and exchanges greetings, but quickly turns the visit into a request for paperwork and ignores the relationship-repair purpose.

GPT-5.2.name: Thomas Wani
GPT-5.2.story:
Thomas had not visited his uncle in Juba for many months. One morning he walked to the compound and called from the gate. His uncle came out slowly and smiled when he recognized him.

"Ah, Thomas," the uncle said.

Thomas shook his hand and asked, "How is your health? How is everyone?" He greeted his aunt and nodded to the children near the doorway. His uncle pointed to a chair and told him to sit.

Thomas sat, but before his uncle finished describing the family news, Thomas pulled a folded form from his pocket. "Uncle, I need your signature as a witness," he said. "That is why I came today."

His uncle looked down at the paper. "You have been away for so long, and now you bring papers?"

Thomas smiled quickly. "We can talk another time. The office closes soon." He placed a pen on the table and tapped the blank line with one finger. While his uncle read, Thomas checked his phone and told his aunt, "I cannot stay for tea."

When the paper was signed, Thomas folded it, stood, and said, "Thank you, Uncle. I will come properly next time." He waved at the children and walked back toward the gate.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: After a long time, the protagonist appears only when they need something and leaves immediately afterward.

GPT-5.2.name: Gatluak Deng
GPT-5.2.story:
Gatluak had not visited his cousin’s home in Juba for a long time. One afternoon he arrived at the compound and walked straight in without carrying anything. He stood near the doorway and called, “Cousin, are you there?”

His cousin came out and greeted him. “Ah, Gatluak. Sit,” he said, pulling a chair.

Gatluak remained standing. “I need your motorbike,” he said. “Just for today.”

His cousin looked at him and asked, “How are you? How is your mother?”

Gatluak waved his hand once. “She is fine,” he said, eyes on the bike parked near the wall. “Give me the key.”

The cousin asked again, “Where have you been? We have not seen you.”

Gatluak replied, “I am busy.” He held out his hand. The cousin brought the key from inside and placed it in Gatluak’s palm.

Gatluak turned immediately, walked to the motorbike, and started it. He did not sit or ask about anyone. As the engine rattled, his cousin stood by the doorway.

Gatluak rode out of the gate, the front wheel lifting slightly over the bump in the road, and he disappeared down the street while dust rose behind him.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When addressing people, the protagonist uses formal titles or kinship terms, especially for elders.

GPT-5.2.name: Nyakim Nyaluak
GPT-5.2.story:
Nyakim arrived at a neighbour’s compound in Juba with her mother for an afternoon visit. Several older relatives sat under a shade cloth, and a young boy brought stools. Nyakim greeted each person as she approached.

“Good afternoon, Mama,” she said to an older woman, bending slightly as she extended her hand.

The older woman nodded. “Afternoon, my daughter,” she replied.

Nyakim turned to an older man with a grey beard and said, “Father, how are you today?”

He lifted his hand in response. “I am fine,” he said.

When her mother introduced another visitor, Nyakim repeated the terms. “Auntie, please sit,” she said, sliding a stool forward. “Uncle, welcome.”

A younger cousin called across the yard, “Nyakim!”

Nyakim looked over and said, “Wait, I’m greeting Baba and Mama first,” then returned her attention to the seated elders.

Tea was brought out, and Nyakim accepted her cup with both hands, saying, “Thank you, Mama.” When a child ran past, she held the cup steady and shifted her stool so an older woman could stretch her legs, and she continued the conversation, addressing the elders as Father, Mama, and Auntie throughout the visit.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they do not know the correct term, the protagonist asks what title to use and then uses it consistently.

GPT-5.2.name: Hawa Musa
GPT-5.2.story:
Hawa visited a home in Wau with a colleague and was introduced to an older man seated near the doorway. Her colleague said, “This is our elder, Mr. Luka.”

Hawa hesitated for a beat and then asked, “Please, what title should I use for you? Should I say Father, or Elder, or Mr. Luka?”

The older man looked at her and replied, “Say Baba Luka.”

Hawa nodded. “Thank you, Baba Luka,” she said, and took the stool offered to her.

During the conversation, whenever she spoke to him, she used the same title. “Baba Luka, may I ask about the meeting time?” she said. When he answered, she responded, “Yes, Baba Luka.”

A younger woman entered with water and greeted him. Hawa watched and then repeated, “Good afternoon, Baba Luka.”

Later, another elder joined, and Hawa turned to her colleague again. “And for him?” she asked quietly.

“Call him Uncle James,” the colleague said.

Hawa stood and greeted, “Good afternoon, Uncle James,” then returned to her seat. As the visit continued, she addressed each older person with the titles she had asked for, and she repeated “Baba Luka” each time she directed a question to him.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist uses the correct title at first, then switches to a casual form of address later.

GPT-5.2.name: Kuol Ayuen
GPT-5.2.story:
Kuol arrived at a family gathering near Bor and greeted the older men seated under a tree. He bent slightly and said, “Good afternoon, Father,” to one elder and “Uncle,” to another, shaking hands in turn.

The elder nodded. “Sit,” he said, pointing to a stool.

Kuol sat and listened while the elders spoke. At first he kept using the titles. “Father, I heard the market will open early,” he said. “Uncle, is that true?”

As the conversation loosened, Kuol began talking with a cousin about football. He laughed and then turned back to the elder mid-sentence. “Peter, you saw that match?” Kuol asked, using the elder’s first name.

The elder looked at him. “Hmm?” he said.

Kuol continued without correcting himself. “Peter, tell them about the team,” he said, waving toward the group.

A cousin nudged Kuol’s knee lightly, but Kuol kept speaking, switching between “Peter” and “Baba” depending on the sentence.

The elder answered briefly and then resumed talking with another older man. Kuol leaned forward, still animated, and called out “Peter” again as he pointed toward the road where a motorbike passed.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist addresses elders by casual names without titles even after being introduced formally.

GPT-5.2.name: Sammy John
GPT-5.2.story:
Sammy followed his friend into a family home near Torit where several elders sat on chairs in the front room. His friend introduced him carefully. “This is Elder Peter,” she said, gesturing. “And this is Mama Rose.”

Sammy waved casually. “Hey Peter,” he said, then looked to the older woman. “Hi Rose.”

The room went quiet for a moment. The friend’s eyebrows lifted slightly.

The older man said, “I am Elder Peter,” stressing the word.

Sammy shrugged. “Yeah, Peter, nice to meet you,” he repeated, dropping onto a chair without waiting to be offered one.

Mama Rose adjusted her shawl and said, “Sit properly.”

Sammy laughed lightly. “Okay, Rose,” he said.

A younger cousin offered tea. Sammy took the cup with one hand and said, “Thanks.” He kept talking to his friend, calling out “Peter” when he disagreed about a story and “Rose” when he asked where the sugar was.

When the friend tried to correct him—“Say Mama”—Sammy waved it off and said, “It’s fine,” then leaned back and stretched his legs toward the centre of the room while the elders continued speaking among themselves.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When an elder is speaking, the protagonist gives full attention and allows the elder to finish before responding.

GPT-5.2.name: Aluel Majok
GPT-5.2.story:
Aluel sat in the front room of her aunt’s home in Juba where an older grandmother figure, Mama Atong, was telling a story to the younger relatives. The room was quiet except for the fan and the occasional clink of a spoon in a tea glass.

Mama Atong spoke slowly, hands resting on her lap. Aluel kept her eyes on Mama Atong’s face and nodded when the older woman paused. A younger cousin whispered something to Aluel, but Aluel lifted a finger gently and stayed still.

Mama Atong continued, describing where people should meet and what time. Aluel did not interrupt. She waited until Mama Atong’s last sentence ended and the older woman leaned back in her chair.

Then Aluel spoke. “Mama, thank you,” she said. “So we meet after the morning prayer, and we go together from here?”

Mama Atong nodded. “Yes,” she said.

Aluel replied, “Okay,” and reached for her notebook to write it down.

Another cousin tried to jump in, but Aluel kept her gaze on Mama Atong, waiting until the elder finished adding a detail about who should carry the water. When Mama Atong stopped, Aluel asked one more question and then fell quiet again, hands folded on her notebook.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they must step away briefly, the protagonist asks permission to excuse themselves and returns to listen afterward.

GPT-5.2.name: Rita Lemi
GPT-5.2.story:
Rita sat on a plastic chair in a courtyard in Wau as an older man, Uncle Gabriel, spoke to the family about a wedding visit. People listened, and Rita kept her hands in her lap, eyes on the elder.

Halfway through, Rita felt her phone vibrating in her pocket with repeated calls. She waited for a short pause, then leaned forward slightly. “Uncle Gabriel,” she said, “may I excuse myself for one minute to take a call?”

The elder looked at her and nodded. “Go,” he said.

Rita stood, walked a few steps away toward the doorway, and answered quietly. “Hello, yes,” she said, turning her back to the group. After a brief exchange, she ended the call and returned to her chair.

Before sitting, she looked at the elder and said, “Thank you.”

Uncle Gabriel continued speaking without restarting. Rita stayed still, listening for what she had missed. When the elder explained the order of greetings, Rita nodded and whispered to a cousin, “What did he say about the time?”

The cousin murmured, “We leave at six.”

Rita nodded once and focused again on Uncle Gabriel until he finished. When the elder ended, Rita asked a single question about where they would meet, then fell quiet as others spoke.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist gives full attention at the start, then begins checking a phone while remaining seated.

GPT-5.2.name: Bol Chol
GPT-5.2.story:
Bol sat with his uncles and a few neighbours near Bor while an elder spoke about plans for a visit to another household. At the start, Bol kept his head up, eyes on the elder, and nodded when the elder mentioned the time and the route.

The elder continued, describing who would greet first and who would carry the items. Bol listened for a few minutes, then his phone buzzed. He slid it from his pocket without standing and lowered it onto his thigh.

While the elder kept speaking, Bol’s thumb moved across the screen. He glanced down, then up, then down again. The elder’s voice stayed steady.

A cousin beside Bol whispered, “Put it away,” but Bol did not answer. He typed quickly, then scrolled.

The elder asked a question to the group. “Do you understand?” he said.

Bol looked up and said, “Yes,” without lifting his phone from his lap.

The elder continued, and Bol returned his gaze to the screen, tapping to open another message. He stayed seated and quiet, but his attention shifted between the elder’s words and the phone.

When the elder finally stopped speaking, Bol locked his phone, slipped it back into his pocket, and looked up as others began asking questions.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist talks over elders and continues side conversations while an elder is speaking.

GPT-5.2.name: Emmanuel Pitia
GPT-5.2.story:
Emmanuel sat with several relatives in a shaded area near Yei as an older elder began giving instructions about a family visit. The elder spoke in a steady voice, pointing toward the path and mentioning the time.

While the elder was still talking, Emmanuel turned to his cousin and said, “Did you see the price of fuel today?”

His cousin answered, and Emmanuel kept the side conversation going. “No, listen, it’s crazy,” he said, laughing, then lifted his phone to show a message.

The elder raised his voice slightly. “I am speaking,” he said.

Emmanuel waved a hand as if to say “one moment” and continued. “And then he said he will come tomorrow,” Emmanuel told his cousin, talking over the elder’s words.

A woman nearby glanced between them. The elder kept speaking, but Emmanuel leaned back and called out, “Wait, elder, you said Saturday or Sunday?” before the elder had finished explaining.

The elder paused, looking annoyed, then repeated himself. Emmanuel interrupted again, “Okay, okay,” and turned back to his cousin, saying, “We can leave early and skip that part.”

As the elder continued, Emmanuel’s laughter popped up in the middle of the elder’s sentences, and he kept whispering to his cousin while tapping on his phone screen.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: On arrival at a home, the protagonist greets the man of the house first before greeting others present.

GPT-5.2.name: Kuol Akol
GPT-5.2.story:
Kuol walked to a friend’s family compound in Juba to deliver a message about a meeting. At the entrance he called out, and a young boy opened the gate. Kuol stepped inside and saw several people seated under a tree, including the father of the house.

Kuol walked directly toward the man and stopped in front of him. He lowered his head slightly and extended his hand. “Good afternoon,” Kuol said. “How are you, Father?”

The man took his hand and replied, “I am fine.”

Only after that did Kuol turn to the others. He greeted the woman sitting nearby. “Mother, how are you?” he asked, shaking her hand. Then he greeted the older uncle on the next chair and the younger men standing near the wall. He greeted the children last, nodding to them as they watched.

Kuol then took the folded note from his pocket and held it in his right hand. He addressed the man of the house again. “I brought this for you,” he said.

The man accepted it, opened it, and read. Kuol waited until the man finished, then asked, “Should I give a reply?” The man spoke briefly, and Kuol nodded.

Kuol said, “Thank you,” shook the man’s hand again, and walked back toward the gate while the boy followed to open it.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the man of the house is not present, the protagonist greets those present and then greets him first when he arrives.

GPT-5.2.name: Achol Maker
GPT-5.2.story:
Achol went to her cousin’s home in Juba to deliver a small parcel. When she entered the compound, she did not see the man of the house. Her cousin’s wife and an older aunt were seated outside.

Achol greeted them first. “Good afternoon, Mother,” she said, shaking the older aunt’s hand. “Sister, how are you?” she asked the cousin’s wife.

They replied and offered her a chair. Achol sat and placed the parcel on her lap. “I came to drop this,” she said.

While they spoke, footsteps approached from the lane, and the man of the house entered the compound carrying a plastic bag. Achol stood immediately and stepped toward him.

“Good afternoon,” she said, extending her hand. “How are you?”

He took her hand and answered. Achol kept her eyes lowered briefly, then said, “I came to see the house and greet.”

After greeting him, Achol returned to the chair and continued the conversation with the women. She then lifted the parcel and addressed the man again. “This is for you,” she said, holding it with her right hand.

He accepted it and placed it on the table. The cousin’s wife poured water into cups.

When Achol prepared to leave, she greeted the man again first, then greeted the women, and walked to the gate while the older aunt called, “Go well,” from her chair.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist greets the man of the house first, but rushes through everyone else's greetings and treats the order as a checklist rather than a respectful exchange.

GPT-5.2.name: Emmanuel Khamis
GPT-5.2.story:
Emmanuel entered a compound in Juba carrying a small bag of bread. He saw the man of the house seated near the doorway, so he walked straight to him first and shook his hand.

"Good afternoon," Emmanuel said. "How are you?"

The man began to answer, but Emmanuel had already turned toward the others. He extended his hand to the woman on the mat and said, "Mother," then pulled his hand back before she finished replying. He tapped hands with two younger men, nodded at the children, and turned toward an older uncle in the corner.

Several people were still speaking their greetings when Emmanuel moved on. The woman started to ask about his family, but he cut across her words with another quick "Yes, yes, everyone is fine." He kept his body angled toward the gate as if measuring how soon he could leave.

The man of the house pointed to a chair and said, "Sit."

Emmanuel placed the bread on the table but stayed standing. "I came to greet," he said, checking his phone as it buzzed. He gave the man another quick handshake, nodded at the group without waiting for their responses, and walked back out while the aunt was still mid-sentence.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist greets others first and does not greet the man of the house.

GPT-5.2.name: Santino Biar
GPT-5.2.story:
Santino arrived at a compound in Juba where his friend’s family was gathered. He walked in and immediately greeted the women sitting on mats. “Hello, mothers,” he said, shaking hands from one to the next.

He then turned to the children and patted one boy on the shoulder. “How are you?” he asked.

A man sat on a chair near the doorway, watching. Santino glanced in his direction but did not greet him. He continued talking to the women, asking about the market and the price of flour.

His friend came out and Santino greeted him loudly, “Ah, my brother!” and shook his hand with both of his.

The man on the chair cleared his throat once. Santino kept speaking to his friend and did not step over to greet the man.

After a few minutes, Santino asked his friend, “Can you help me carry these sacks tomorrow?” He leaned closer and spoke quickly.

The man stood up and walked past them toward the yard. Santino’s eyes followed him for a moment, then returned to his friend.

Santino adjusted his bag strap and said, “I will come early in the morning,” then turned and walked back toward the gate without greeting the man as he passed behind him.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In communities where it is practiced, the protagonist kneels when greeting or serving someone of much higher status.

GPT-5.2.name: Nyakuoth Deng
GPT-5.2.story:
Nyakuoth attended a gathering in Juba where an older respected leader from her community was visiting a family home. People arranged chairs in the yard, and younger women carried trays of tea.

When Nyakuoth approached the leader to greet him, she stopped a step away and lowered herself to her knees on the packed earth. With her head slightly bowed, she greeted him softly. “Good afternoon,” she said.

The leader answered, and Nyakuoth remained kneeling as she extended a tray with two hands. The cup sat steady on the tray. She held it up at a comfortable height for him to take.

The leader reached out and took the cup. Nyakuoth waited on her knees until his hand released the tray’s edge. Then she shifted her knees back, still low, and turned slightly to the side.

A woman behind her whispered, “Give the sugar too.” Nyakuoth, still kneeling, picked up the small sugar bowl from the tray and held it out with both hands. The leader took a spoonful.

When the leader finished greeting another visitor, Nyakuoth remained low until he nodded at her, and only then did she rise smoothly to her feet.

She carried the empty tray to the side, placed it on a stool, and returned to kneel again as she served the next cup to another elder seated nearby.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because kneeling is physically difficult, the protagonist bows or lowers their body in another respectful way while serving.

GPT-5.2.name: Akec Nhial
GPT-5.2.story:
Akec’s knee was stiff from an old injury, but she was helping at a family gathering in Juba where an elder of much higher status was seated at the front. When she carried a tray of tea toward him, she moved carefully.

As she reached the elder, she did not drop fully to her knees. Instead, she lowered her body in another way: she bent at the knees into a deep squat, keeping her back straight and her head slightly bowed. Her hands held the tray with both hands, steady at chest height.

“Good afternoon,” she said, voice soft.

The elder replied, and Akec stayed low in the squat, eyes down. She extended the tray forward. The elder took the cup.

Akec then lowered the tray slightly and bowed her upper body again, still keeping her weight low. She offered the sugar bowl with both hands. The elder took sugar and nodded.

A woman next to Akec whispered, “Are you okay?” Akec gave a small nod while remaining low.

When the elder finished, Akec shifted her feet and rose slowly, using her thigh muscles rather than dropping to her knees. She stepped backward one pace before turning.

She carried the tray to the side table, set it down, rubbed her knee briefly with her palm, and then returned to the group, moving carefully as she approached another elder and lowered herself into the same squat to serve.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist kneels to greet or serve, but immediately stands over the elder and presses their own agenda before the elder can respond.

GPT-5.2.name: Lual Atem
GPT-5.2.story:
Lual attended a community visit in Juba where an older, higher-status man was seated under a tree. Younger men were passing tea, and Lual carried a cup and saucer toward the elder.

He dropped to one knee and greeted him. "Good afternoon," Lual said, holding the cup forward with both hands. The elder received it and began to reply.

Before the elder finished speaking, Lual pushed himself up from the kneeling position and stood directly above him. "I also brought the message from the youth group," he said, leaning forward.

The elder looked up with the cup still in his hand. Lual continued, pointing toward the road. "They will come tomorrow. You should tell them where to meet."

A man beside the elder lifted his hand as if to slow him down. Lual did not pause. He kept talking while standing, shifting his feet close to the elder's chair. When the elder asked a question, Lual answered quickly and added another instruction.

After that, Lual turned to greet another person, still upright, leaving the elder holding the tea while the conversation moved around him.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In the same context, the protagonist refuses to kneel and remains standing while serving.

GPT-5.2.name: Yolanda Poni
GPT-5.2.story:
Yolanda helped at a community gathering in Juba where elders sat on chairs under a tarp. A respected elder was seated at the front, and younger women moved around with tea.

When it was Yolanda’s turn to serve, she walked straight up to the elder and remained standing close to him. She held the tray at her waist and said, “Here,” without lowering herself.

An older woman nearby gestured downward with her hand, indicating kneeling. Yolanda shook her head once and kept standing.

The elder reached for the cup, and Yolanda tilted the tray slightly to make it easier, still standing. A man seated beside the elder looked at Yolanda and then looked away.

Someone behind Yolanda whispered, “Kneel.” Yolanda replied quietly, “No,” and shifted her weight onto one leg.

She remained upright while the elder took the cup. Then she held out the sugar bowl, still standing. The elder took sugar and set the spoon down.

Yolanda turned her body and walked away without kneeling at any point. As she passed the older woman, the older woman lifted her eyebrows and clicked her tongue once.

Yolanda set the tray down on a table and stood with her arms folded while another server moved forward and knelt to greet the elder.
